settings.headline: The bag you
settings.description: One bag from the bakery to the studio to dinner. Four hundred grams empty. Ten kilos without complaint. The rest of your bags can stay in the cupboard.

settings.label: Volume
settings.description: Laptop, yoga mat, groceries, beach kit, a stack of paints — whatever the day demands, fits without pulling the bag out of shape.

settings.label: The Strap
settings.description: Wide enough to spread the load across your shoulder. At seven kilos, worn crossbody, you stop noticing it. At ten, you still walk home.

settings.label: Inner Label
settings.description: The turquoise label is the only colour on the bag. Signed, dated and numbered by the person who sewed it — so they can find it again, and so can you.

settings.label: Dimensions
settings.description: Slim against the body when empty. Folds into a handbag, a drawer, a bike basket. The only bag you own that doesn't ask for its own cupboard.

settings.headline: From film
settings.copy_1: We came up through the film industry — where function isn't a feeling, it's a requirement. A strap either holds for twelve hours on set, or it doesn't. A case either survives the rain in Hamburg, or it doesn't. You learn fast that good design is the kind you stop noticing.
settings.quote: Would I carry this bag myself — with maximum standards for function and design?
settings.copy_2: We made thirty-one prototypes of the Zuuma Bag. We kept the one we stopped noticing. Berlin, by hand, numbered — because the person who sewed it should be able to find it again.

settings.title: Zuuma
settings.copy: Monday it's a laptop and a lunch. Wednesday a yoga mat, a bottle of wine, the dry-cleaning. Saturday the market — two kilos of tomatoes, bread under one arm, flowers under the other. Deep black cotton canvas, screen-printed in Berlin, signed inside. The bag stops being a thing you carry. It becomes the day you had.

settings.sub: Same canvas, same hands. Made for the hours nobody sees.

settings.headline: Worn indoors,
settings.subline: What you put on when the door is closed and the work begins. Kitchen, studio, darkroom, workbench.

settings.label: Material
settings.description: The same canvas as the bag, dyed deep black. Olive oil, tomato, gesso, espresso, turmeric — it takes them all and washes hot without losing shape.

settings.label: Fit
settings.description: Adjustable neck strap, front-knotted waist ties over a metre long. Sits the same on a 1.60 m baker and a 1.95 m painter — the apron adapts, you don't.

settings.label: Detail
settings.description: Numbered, signed, made in Berlin. Same signature as the bag — so you know exactly what you are wearing.

settings.title: Zuuma
settings.copy: Friday night it's four onions and a red wine stain. Sunday it's oil paint on the left thigh where you always wipe the brush. Tuesday it's flour at the elbow from a loaf that didn't rise. Heavy black canvas, long ties that wrap and knot at the front, two pockets deep enough for a phone, a pen, a wooden spoon. It learns your hands. Wash it hot, wear it harder — the apron looks better the year after you bought it than the day it arrived.
settings.spec_line: Heavy canvas · One size · Long front-tie · Two deep pockets
settings.note: Numbered · Handmade in Berlin · Hot wash, line dry

settings.title: For the love
settings.standfirst: The bag and the Schürze are the Workshop — built, tested, numbered. The Studio is what we make when nothing has to be solved. Pictures we couldn't not make, printed on paper that outlives the wall it hangs on. Some things don't need a reason — they're just what the hands do when the day is done.

settings.name: Palm Noir
settings.sub: A palm at 2 a.m., shot on the way home from a shoot.

settings.name: Nocturnal Garden
settings.sub: A Berlin courtyard the week nothing bloomed. Black on black, green if you look twice.

settings.name: Untitled No. 3
settings.sub: Fifty signed, numbered, and gone. One wall per country, roughly.
settings.note: Drops this season — first fifty names on the list get first refusal.

settings.headline: Bag &
settings.copy: The two things we put on before the day starts. One goes on the shoulder, one gets tied at the waist — same black canvas, same turquoise label, same pair of hands in Kreuzberg. Bought together, they arrive in one box with sequential numbers. €18 back for keeping them together.
settings.note: One box, two numbers, sequential. Berlin.

settings.question: How much can the Zuuma Bag carry?
settings.answer: 30 litres, rated to 10 kg, stress-tested well past it. At 7 kg on the crossbody strap you forget it's there. The strap is the last thing to fail, and it won't be soon.

settings.question: Is it suitable for all body types and heights?
settings.answer: The strap adjusts to 132 cm — equally at home on someone 1.60 m and someone over 1.90 m. One bag, many bodies, by design.

settings.question: Can I fold it when not in use?
settings.answer: Absolutely. Unlike most tote bags of this size, the Zuuma Bag folds flat and fits inside a handbag or a bicycle basket. It's designed to be part of your everyday — not an obstacle to it.

settings.question: How do I care for the bag?
settings.answer: The screen print is washable up to 30 degrees. We recommend washing inside out on a gentle cycle. The 100% cotton canvas gets better with age — it's built for real life use, not careful handling.

settings.question: Are the prints shipped separately?
settings.answer: Yes. Palm Noir, Nocturnal Garden and the upcoming limited edition are printed and shipped on demand via Prodigi — ensuring the highest print quality and a lower environmental footprint.

settings.question: Will there be more colours or editions?
settings.answer: Yes. We're exploring olive green as the next colour. Future editions may also be tied to specific events or charity collaborations. Each new colour will justify itself — by the work, not the packaging.
